description_long: This object consists of two standard orange traffic cones with alternating white and orange horizontal stripes. Both cones have a conical shape with a hollow tip and are mounted on square bases that are slightly wider than the cone itself. The cones and their bases show visible signs of dirt and wear, particularly around the lower sections, suggesting prolonged outdoor use. The white stripes are broad and evenly spaced, providing high visibility, while the orange sections are slightly faded in some areas. The bases appear to be made of a heavier material to provide stability, and the cones themselves are likely made of flexible plastic or rubber. No logos, text, or additional markings are visible on the cones.
description: A pair of orange and white striped traffic cones with square bases, showing dirt and wear, designed for high visibility and stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing two cones side by side, with clear visibility of the alternating orange and white stripes and dirt accumulation at the base.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the conical shape, hollow tips, and the square bases with visible dirt and wear.
description_view_2: Rear view of the cones, showing the same stripe pattern and dirt, with the bases slightly offset from each other.
description_view_3: Side angle emphasizing the hollow tips, the broad white stripes, and the dirty, worn bases.
attribute_reflective_stripes: Each cone has three wide, evenly spaced white reflective stripes encircling the cone horizontally.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is hollow, circular, and tapers to a rounded point, colored orange with no attachments.
attribute_base_design: The base is square, flat, and relatively thick, made of a heavier material for stability, with visible dirt and wear patterns.
attribute_presence_of_logos_or_markings: No logos or markings are visible.
attribute_condition: Worn and dirty, with visible signs of outdoor use and some fading on the orange sections.
